_about: v0.2.8 — zh-CN structured sample. Same paper as matched, but target_language/ui_language are zh-CN. The renderer must show Chinese labels (解析状态, 置信度, 匹配论文, 匹配 arXiv ID) and the legacy list stays in place for back-compat.

source_resolution: legacy list kept for back-compat with v0.2.5 samples

steps: Input string: 'Attention Is All You Need'.
Resolver status: matched; match_type: title; confidence: high.
Matched resolver hint: 'Attention Is All You Need' → Attention Is All You Need (arXiv 1706.03762)
Resolver step: cli overlay via p3pr paper_title subcommand.
source_resolution.source_resolution_step: cli overlay via p3pr paper_title subcommand

summaries.one_sentence: Attention Is All You Need introduces the Transformer, a sequence model that relies entirely on self-attention and removes recurrence and convolutions.
three_sentence: The Transformer uses multi-head scaled dot-product self-attention to model token relationships in parallel.
It removes recurrence and convolutions, enabling much faster training and better long-range modelling on translation and other tasks.
On WMT 2014 En-De and En-Fr translation the Transformer surpasses prior encoder-decoder baselines while using a fraction of the training compute.
ten_sentence: The paper proposes the Transformer, a new sequence transduction architecture.
The core building block is multi-head scaled dot-product self-attention.
Self-attention allows every position to attend to every other position in O(1) sequential steps.
Encoder and decoder are stacks of identical layers with attention and position-wise feed-forward sub-layers.
Sinusoidal positional encodings inject order information.
Training uses Adam with a warmup-then-decay learning rate schedule.
Label smoothing is applied to the output projection.
On WMT 2014 En-De the base model reaches 27.5 BLEU and the big model 28.4 BLEU.
On WMT 2014 En-Fr the big model reaches 41.8 BLEU, a single-model state of the art at the time.
The architecture has since become the foundation of most large language models.

five_cs.context: Sequence transduction before this work was dominated by RNN/LSTM encoder-decoders with attention; training was slow and long-range dependencies were hard to learn.
five_cs.correctness: The mathematics of scaled dot-product attention is correct; the paper's experimental claims about training cost and BLEU are reproducible on standard WMT data.
contributions: Introduces the Transformer architecture based purely on attention.
Introduces multi-head attention as a way to attend to different representation subspaces jointly.
Demonstrates large BLEU gains on WMT 2014 translation at a fraction of training cost.
five_cs.clarity: The paper is clearly written with strong diagrams and a tight empirical section.

pass1.context: Pre-2017 sequence-to-sequence work used recurrent and convolutional encoder-decoders; this paper proposes an attention-only alternative.
pass1.correctness: Math and experiments are sound; results have been reproduced widely.
pass1.decision: Worth deep-reading as a foundational paper.

main_ideas: Use multiple attention heads in parallel.
Train with Adam + warmup + label smoothing.
pass2.method_summary: Encoder-decoder with stacked self-attention and feed-forward layers, residual connections, layer norm, sinusoidal positional encodings, multi-head attention with d_model=512, 8 heads in the base model.
figures_tables: Table 1: maximum path length comparisons across layer types.
Table 2: WMT 2014 BLEU and training cost.

key_references.title: Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate
Sequence to Sequence Learning with Neural Networks

method_reconstruction: Tokenize input and embed with d_model=512.
Apply N=6 encoder layers; each layer is multi-head self-attention + position-wise FFN with residual + LayerNorm.
Decoder is similar with masked self-attention and encoder-decoder attention.
Linear + softmax projection to vocabulary, label smoothing 0.1.
critical_review: Quadratic memory in sequence length is the main scaling concern.
Sinusoidal positions were later replaced by RoPE / ALiBi in many follow-ups.
Label smoothing slightly hurts perplexity but improves BLEU and calibration.
hidden_assumptions: GPU/TPU memory large enough for full-sequence softmax.

claims_evidence_map.claim: Self-attention is competitive with recurrence on translation.
claims_evidence_map.evidence: [Paper evidence] Table 2 shows 28.4 BLEU on WMT 2014 En-De with the big Transformer.
claims_evidence_map.label: [Paper evidence]

figures_tables.explanation: Encoder-decoder overview: stacked attention and feed-forward blocks.
Maximum path length per layer type, motivating attention.
BLEU and training cost on WMT 2014.
Ablations: heads, d_model, attention type, label smoothing.

glossary.definition: Mechanism letting every position in a sequence attend to every other position.
Parallel application of several attention heads to capture different subspaces.
Sinusoidal vector added to token embeddings to encode order.
Regularization that softens one-hot targets with a uniform mixture.

limitations: Sinusoidal positions later outperformed by RoPE / ALiBi.

steps: Train base model for 100k steps, big model for 300k steps.
Average last 5 checkpoints, beam size 4, length penalty 0.6.
sanity_checks: BLEU on dev set crosses 25 within 50k steps.
success_criteria: ≥ 27 BLEU on WMT 2014 En-De with the base model.

open_questions: Can attention replace recurrence entirely at the trillion-token scale?
What is the best positional encoding scheme for long contexts?
final_checklist: I can describe scaled dot-product attention in one paragraph.
I can describe multi-head attention in one paragraph.
I can list the four key design choices of the Transformer.
I can name the two main empirical results on WMT 2014.
I can articulate the quadratic-memory limitation.
